title: Daily Briefing — Friday, June 5
tldr: A quieter day — the week's big moves (Trump EO, Microsoft Build, the AI Act draft, Anthropic's S-1) all landed Jun 1–4; Friday was the market digesting them.
Colorado's AI Act takes effect June 30 — and the new federal “Great American AI Act” draft would freeze it on the development side. The state-vs-federal collision is now the thing to watch.
A Claude Sonnet 4.8 leak keeps gaining credibility — treat as rumor until Anthropic confirms, but a mid-tier refresh would reset the price/performance map again.
NVIDIA signaled a push to “reinvent the Windows PC” around on-device AI — the on-device/edge thread continues.

blocks.sub: Slow news day — and that's worth naming
blocks.p: After a packed Jun 1–4 (Anthropic's S-1, Trump's AI EO, Microsoft's in-house MAI models, the Great American AI Act draft, MiniMax M3), Friday was mostly second-day analysis rather than new launches. No major model shipped.
blocks.why: Most of the value on a quiet day is consolidation: the smart move is to act on the week's big shifts, not chase a thin headline.
blocks.doIt: Use quiet days to do the homework the busy days created — today that means reading the actual Great American AI Act provisions if you're >$500M revenue, or benchmarking a cheap model while nothing new is competing for your attention.

blocks.p: Colorado's AI Act is set to take effect June 30; California's frontier-transparency rules are live. The Obernolte–Trahan federal draft (Jun 4) would preempt state laws that regulate model development for three years — but explicitly not deployment. Friday was largely legal teams parsing which of their obligations survive.
blocks.why: This is the practical fault line for anyone building on AI in the US: your fine-tuning/training pipeline may fall on the (preempted) development side while your shipped product stays under state deployment law.
blocks.doIt: Draw the development-vs-deployment boundary on your own stack now, so you know which obligations are federal-ceiling'd and which remain state-by-state.

blocks.sub: Rumor watch: Claude Sonnet 4.8 + NVIDIA's Windows PC play
blocks.p: A Claude Sonnet 4.8 leak gained traction (unconfirmed). Separately, NVIDIA signaled an initiative to “reinvent the Windows PC” around local AI acceleration.
blocks.why: A Sonnet refresh would reprice the mid-tier (the workhorse for most production traffic); NVIDIA's PC push is another data point that inference is migrating toward the edge.
blocks.note: Both are directional — leak + signal, not shipped product. Don't re-architect on a rumor; just keep your eval harness ready to re-run when Sonnet 4.8 actually lands.

blocks.sub: Keep a “re-run on release” eval kit ready
blocks.p: Weeks like this one show models reprice fast and often. The teams that benefit aren't the ones who read every leak — they're the ones who can drop a new model into a standing eval the day it ships and get a verdict in an hour. Maintain 3–5 of your real tasks as a fixed, scored eval set so any new model (Sonnet 4.8, MiniMax M3 weights, DeepSeek V4) gets an objective, same-day yes/no instead of vibes.

sources: buildfastwithai (Jun 5 roundup) · NeuralBuddies (Jun 5 recap) · Roll Call / Axios (Great American AI Act; Colorado AI Act effective Jun 30) · The Decoder (Sonnet 4.8 leak chatter) · NVIDIA. Quiet-day briefing; unconfirmed items flagged as directional.
